exemplar.title: Building a remote-controlled car with FPV camera view on head tracking gimbal.

detailedExemplarData.title: Building a remote-controlled car with FPV camera view on head tracking gimbal.

strengths: Use of trial-and-error iterations in Fusion 360 showcasing hands-on skill development
Thorough source evaluation using the CARRDS framework to support research quality
Visual documentation (e.g., steering mechanism photo) illustrating application of design skills
weaknesses: Research questions are too broad and need prioritization to target critical Fusion 360 skills
Insufficient explicit linkage between selected thinking strategies and specific design challenges
Limited reflection on how each iteration was evaluated for success
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The work includes relevant examples of ATL skills in action, such as iterative modeling and structured source analysis, which strengthen both research and design. To deepen this criterion, the student should refine and prioritize their research questions, explicitly connect chosen critical and creative thinking strategies to the challenges they encountered, and document the criteria used to assess the success of each Fusion 360 iteration.

strengths: Strong connection between personal interest and project topic
Comprehensive set of success criteria informed by market standards
Clear outline of development stages with deadlines
weaknesses: Learning goal lacks measurable, specific success criteria
Some success criteria (e.g., âride dynamicallyâ) are ambiguous and need quantifiable metrics
Plan table omits explicit listing of required resources, dependencies, and clear links from each stage back to the learning goal
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student demonstrates a solid grasp of project scope by tying their personal drone experience to the design and by defining multiple relevant success criteria. The staging of tasks and deadlines is well structured. To elevate this work, the learning goal and criteria should be reframed into measurable milestones, ambiguous terms should be clarified with numeric targets, and the plan should specify resources, task dependencies, and how each development stage advances the stated learning goal.

strengths: Honest reflection on growth through trial and error demonstrating self-awareness
Integration of peer survey feedback to evaluate aesthetics and functionality
Clear product description linking features to design intentions
weaknesses: Product evaluation lacks precise quantitative data (e.g., lag percentages, steering angle errors) to substantiate development needs
Reflection does not explicitly identify which ATL skills were strengthened
One-point rubric examples are not tied to concrete test outcomes or metrics
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflective section provides a candid appraisal of product strengths and limitations, enriched by survey insights. To enhance this reflection, the student should incorporate specific data points to support each identified issue, name and analyze the ATL skills they improved, and anchor their rubric comments to concrete evidence from testing outcomes.

annotations.comment: Strong connection between personal interest and project topic; consider specifying how your prior drone experience will inform specific design decisions to deepen this link.
Trial and error in Fusion 360 is a strong example; add a brief note on how you evaluated the success of each iteration to demonstrate systematic skill application.
Learning goal is clear but lacks measurable success criteria; define specific milestones (e.g., âcomplete three Fusion 360 tutorials and design two functional partsâ).
Your product success criteria are comprehensive but some terms (e.g., âride dynamicallyâ) remain vague; add quantifiable metrics or thresholds for each criterion.
The plan table outlines tasks and deadlines effectively; enhance this by listing required resources and dependencies for each stage to improve feasibility.
You describe using CARRDS to evaluate sources; extend this by reflecting on how this research method improved your efficiency in achieving the learning goal.
Your CARRDS evaluation shows thorough source analysis; apply a similar structured evaluation when selecting Fusion 360 tutorials to ensure consistent learning quality.
You describe three development stages clearly; strengthen your plan by connecting each stage back to your learning goal and showing how they build on personal interest.
The rationale for criteria demonstrates awareness of market standards; link each criterion explicitly to your product design choices to justify its inclusion.
Research questions cover a wide range of topics; prioritize and refine them to target the most critical information needed for your Fusion 360 skill development.
Your photo of the steering mechanism illustrates product development; annotate how this design meets the Movement success criterion to clarify its impact.
Good identification of Thinking Skills as your primary ATL; explicitly link specific critical or creative thinking strategies to the design challenges you faced.
The screenshot of your 3D model shows progress; consider labeling design iterations and noting adjustments to demonstrate development over time.
Your reflection captures growth through trial and error; deepen this by naming specific ATL skills you improved (e.g., perseverance, information literacy).
Overall evaluation is candid about product limitations; integrate more commentary on personal learning gains alongside product critique to balance reflection.
Noting survey use is positive; connect these feedback insights back to your personal learning goals to illustrate how peer input shaped your process.
Product Evaluation section lists strengths and grows; include precise data (e.g., percent lag, degrees of steering inaccuracy) to support each area for development.
Your one-point rubric effectively highlights issues; strengthen it by adding concrete examples (e.g., specific test outcomes) against each criterion.
Survey excerpts provide useful evidence; weave these numerical results into your narrative to directly back up your evaluation statements.
Secondary survey chart reinforces aesthetics success; explain how this positive feedback will guide your future design refinements.
Your product description is clear; directly reference how each feature fulfills corresponding success criteria to create a cohesive evaluation.
